title: Fix: RFC3339 loses sub-second precision, breaking incremental indexing
description:
High-severity bug: formatTime in queries.go uses time.RFC3339 (second precision) to store file_mod_time. Filesystem modtimes have nanosecond precision. After round-trip through SQLite, the comparison existing.FileModTime.Equal(f.FileModTime) always returns false because the stored time is truncated to seconds. This means every file is re-parsed on every run — incremental indexing is effectively broken.

Also fix: parseTime should handle the new format.
requirements: In internal/db/queries.go: change timeFormat from time.RFC3339 to time.RFC3339Nano so sub-second filesystem modtimes survive the SQLite round-trip
Update parseTime to try RFC3339Nano first, then fall back to RFC3339 for backwards compatibility with existing DB entries
Add a test that verifies: store a session with a nanosecond-precision FileModTime, read it back, and confirm FileModTime.Equal returns true
Verify the project compiles with 'go build ./cmd/ccs/' and all tests pass with 'go test ./...'
